title: WORKDIR instruction should only be used with absolute path
description: WORKDIR instruction should only be used with absolute pathAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalreliabilitymaintainabilityCode SmellWORKDIRinstructions should be used with an absolute path for clarity and reliability.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoTheWORKDIRinstruction defines the working directory for anyRUN,CMD,ENTRYPOINT,COPY, andADDinstructions that follow in the Dockerfile.Using absolute paths helps to avoid any confusion or unexpected behavior that could arise, for example after changing the current directory in one of the previous instructions.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since10.7Analyze code in youron-premise CIDe
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: WORKDIR instruction should only be used with absolute path. Using relative paths like 'relative/path', './app', or '../parent' can lead to confusion and unexpected behavior. Use absolute paths starting with '/' for clarity and reliability, such as '/app' or '/var/www/html'.
